resources.string: A small SRS app focused on reading kanji words and expressions in sentences.
Choose the reading of the highlighted kanji or expression in context. Only fast, stable answers count toward Mastered.
The Kanji Trainer app code is distributed under the MIT License.
The bundled kanji and card seed data is treated as a separate data work from the code. Public releases distribute it under Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International (CC BY-SA 4.0).
Kanji readings, classification, and visual component checks may reference public EDRDG-family materials: KANJIDIC2, RADKFILE/KRADFILE, and JMdict/EDICT documentation.

EDRDG materials are provided under Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0. This app does not imply endorsement or warranty by EDRDG or its contributors.\n\nhttps://www.edrdg.org/edrdg/licence.html
If future releases import or adapt sentences from the Tanaka Corpus or Tatoeba, their sources and licenses will be shown in release notes and on this screen.

This app focuses on quickly recognizing the readings of kanji words and expressions inside sentences.
Recommended level: learners who have started building an ear for Japanese and can read hiragana and katakana.
